scenario: Junction insurer adds a new Season input column to a Camunda 8.8 DMN dish-selection table that runs 4000 claims per day. The modeler must give the column a unique technical identifier the engine uses to reference the input clause, and asks which XML attribute on which element carries that identifier.
question: Which XML attribute on which element carries the unique technical identifier of a decision table input clause?

options.text: The label attribute on the input XML element carries the unique technical identifier that Camunda uses to reference the input clause when evaluating the decision table
The id attribute on the parent decisionTable XML element carries the unique technical identifier that Camunda uses to reference each input clause in the decision table
The id attribute on the child inputExpression XML element carries the unique technical identifier that Camunda uses to reference the input clause across the decision table
The id attribute on the input XML element carries the unique technical identifier that Camunda uses to reference the input clause across the deployed decision table

a.text: Incorrect. The label attribute on the input element is a short description of the input, not the technical identifier; the identifier lives on the id attribute of the same input element.

b.text: Incorrect. The id attribute on the decisionTable element identifies the table itself, not its individual input clauses; each input clause has its own id attribute on the input element.

c.text: Incorrect. The inputExpression child element has its own id attribute that identifies the expression, not the input clause; the input clause id lives on the parent input element.

d.text: Correct. The Input ID section states that the input ID is set as the id attribute of the input XML element and is used by Camunda to reference the input clause, and that it is required.

explanation: The Input ID section says the input ID is a unique identifier of the decision table input. It is used by Camunda to reference the input clause, is required, and is set on the id attribute of the input XML element.
